title: Is it safe to have filler after radiofrequency treatments？ | Dr Tim's Tips
filename: Is it safe to have filler after radiofrequency treatments？ ｜ Dr Tim_s Tips.en.vtt
excerpt: So is it safe for your patients to have injectable treatments after a high food treatment that's a radio frequency treatment usually to cause skin tightening [Music] the answer is yes so long as you've given it a decent amount of time you wouldn't want to be doing any procedures …

segments.text: so is it safe for your patients to have injectable treatments after a high food treatment that's a radio frequency treatment usually to cause skin tightening [Music] the answer is yes so long as you've given it a decent amount of time you wouldn't want to be doing any procedures while there's any recovery to be done from a previous procedure that's a general rule for everything that's done on the face if there's any inflammation left over wait usually that's a safe length of time is about two weeks so if you've left a two week gap it's not going to cause any interaction or problems with a dermal filler or a toxin treatment if you've had a radio frequency treatment that long ago so you shouldn't do it on the same day just keep it simple you're going to have some information from whichever procedure you're done and it's just easier to keep things on separated in time so that you know what's going on this is all about simplicity often that's the case with all sorts of things we do it's just simpler to keep it separate even if you think there's probably no interaction likely it makes
things a lot easier for your patients if they do have any side effects and that's why i'd recommend that to weak up hope that helps you

transcript: so is it safe for your patients to have injectable treatments after a high food treatment that's a radio frequency treatment usually to cause skin tightening [Music] the answer is yes so long as you've given it a decent amount of time you wouldn't want to be doing any procedures while there's any recovery to be done from a previous procedure that's a general rule for everything that's done on the face if there's any inflammation left over wait usually that's a safe length of time is about two weeks so if you've left a two week gap it's not going to cause any interaction or problems with a dermal filler or a toxin treatment if you've had a radio frequency treatment that long ago so you shouldn't do it on the same day just keep it simple you're going to have some information from whichever procedure you're done and it's just easier to keep things on separated in time so that you know what's going on this is all about simplicity often that's the case with all sorts of things we do it's just simpler to keep it separate even if you think there's probably no interaction likely it makes things a lot easier for your patients if they do have any side effects and that's why i'd recommend that to weak up hope that helps you
